evals.name: search-then-read-fulltext
evals.prompt: I want to know what The New York Times is currently reporting about artificial intelligence in the workplace. Find their most relevant recent article and give me a short summary of its key points.
evals.expected_output: Uses nyt to search/browse (articlesearch or topstories technology), extracts the article URL (likely via --json), then runs `nyt read <url>` to fetch the full body and summarizes from the actual text — not from the abstract alone and not by scraping nytimes.com directly.

evals.name: books-bestseller-list
evals.prompt: What books are topping the NYT hardcover fiction best-seller list right now? Give me the top 3 with their authors.
evals.expected_output: Runs `nyt books list hardcover-fiction` (discovering the encoded list name via `nyt books lists` if needed) and reports the top 3 titles + authors. Does not need the read/cookie path.

evals.name: read-specific-url
evals.prompt: Can you pull up this NYT article and tell me the main takeaways? https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/23/technology/meta-prediction-markets-app.html
evals.expected_output: Recognizes a specific nytimes.com URL calls for `nyt read <url>` (the cookie-authenticated full-text path), fetches the body, and reports takeaways from the real article text. If the cookie is expired it relays the refresh instruction rather than scraping or hallucinating.
